crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Education-loan AUM of NBFCs seen up ~20% despite slower US biz
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: For non-banking finance companies (NBFCs), growth in education-loan assets under management (AUM) is projected to stay steady at ~20% this fiscal (Chart 1 in annexure), with increasing diversification across study destinations offsetting the impact on demand for US-focused education from policy unce

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: NBFC originations drive securitisation volume to first-quarter peak
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: The securitisation  market heralded its strongest-ever first-quarter volume with issuances surging 22% on-year to a peak of ~Rs 60,000 crore in April-June 2026, compared with the same period last fiscal (Chart 1 in annexure).

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Specialty chemical makers set to catalyse ~6% revenue growth
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Revenue growth of specialty chemical manufacturers is expected to moderate 200 bps  this fiscal from ~8% each in the past two fiscals. Firm domestic demand will support growth but export weakness—driven by supply disruptions and cautious overseas procurement—will cap the overall pace, with trade flo

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: For MFIs, growth gathering pace, AUM to surge ~20% this fiscal
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Growth in assets under management (AUM) of microfinance institutions (MFIs)  is set to accelerate to ~20% this fiscal from 4% last fiscal. Their AUM comprises two businesses, primarily—the microfinance portfolio and non-microfinance.

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: AUM growth of affordable-housing lenders seen steady at 19-20%
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Growth in assets under management (AUM) of affordable housing finance companies  (A-HFCs) is expected to remain healthy at 19-20% this fiscal and the next, in line with the ~19% growth last fiscal. (Chart 1 in annexure).

crisil-research.title: The quiet exit
crisil-research.description: What makes HNIs stay invested? A Crisil study across wealth managers, private banks, and boutique advisors is revealing patterns that challenge common industry assumptions. RM attrition is not a people problem but a structural one. When RMs leave, clients fragment quietly.

crisil-research.title: Crisil Economy First Cut: Sharp spurt
crisil-research.description: For the first time since January 2025, India’s retail inflation rose past the Reserve Bank of India’s (RBI) marker line of 4% in June with the Consumer Price Index (CPI) printing 4.4%, compared with 3.9% in May.

crisil-research.title: Quickonomics: Water, water, every where. But not a lot to grin
crisil-research.description: July’s rains have brought a welcome reprieve after an arid June, easing some of the immediate anxieties surrounding the progress of the southwest monsoon this year. Many parts of India saw heavy rainfall; there were episodes of landslides and life came to a standstill in a raft of locations.  Yet, d

crisil-research.title: RRR: Roti Rice Rate
crisil-research.description: The average cost of preparing a thali at home is calculated based on input prices prevailing in north, south, east and west India. The monthly change reflects the impact on the common man’s expenditure. The data also reveals the ingredients (cereals, pulses, broilers, vegetables, spices, edible oil

crisil-research.title: Indian Economy: El Niño bend to a Strait story
crisil-research.description: Frenetic diplomacy seems to have surmounted confrontation with a sixty-day truce announced between the United States and Iran. Our macroeconomic outlook, which was predicated on about four months of conflict beginning February-end, is being retained.

baroda-etrade-str.title: Building Materials: Q1FY27 Preview – Mixed demand amid commodity inflation
baroda-etrade-str.description: PVC resin price moderation triggers destocking; non agri demand offsets weak agri

baroda-etrade-str.title: Real Estate (Developers): Q1FY27 Preview – Late-cycle consolidation; fundamental demand drivers intact
baroda-etrade-str.description: Lower sales (-6% YoY), launches (+7% YoY) continued to outpace sales; leading to a build-up of inventories (+10% YoY) across top 7 cities

baroda-etrade-str.title: Pharmaceuticals: Q1FY27 Preview – Growth visible despite product concentration in the base
baroda-etrade-str.description: Moderate quarter expected due to gRevlimid in the base. No impact of the Middle-East war seen by the companies in 1QFY27

baroda-etrade-str.title: Cement: Q1FY27 Preview – Q1 impacted by higher operational cost; demand steady
baroda-etrade-str.description: Cement demand is expected to remain intact in Q1FY27. We expect coverage company demand to growth at ~11%YoY higher than industry

baroda-etrade-str.title: Retail: Q1FY27 Preview – Broad-based Recovery
baroda-etrade-str.description: Jewellery demand resilient, but Q1 a test of the quality of earnings rather than headline growth

baroda-etrade-str.title: Oil & Gas: Q1FY27 Preview – Mixed performance expected amid elevated crude & LNG prices
baroda-etrade-str.description: Volatile crude & LNG prices are likely to weigh on performance. Negative Implications for OMCs and Gas Utility Companies

baroda-etrade-str.title: Insurance: Q1FY27 Preview – Broad-based growth continues; LICI placed on a low base
baroda-etrade-str.description: For our coverage companies, including LICI, APE growth for the quarter is expected to be healthy at 12% YoY

baroda-etrade-str.title: IT Services: ‘Show me the growth’ phase will keep valuations compressed
baroda-etrade-str.description: Nifty IT PE derated CYTD (~35%) due to concerns on long term growth. AI reinvention services hold out hope. We remain skeptical

baroda-etrade-str.title: Banking: Q1FY27 Preview – Credit momentum builds; resilient AQ supports earnings
baroda-etrade-str.description: Credit growth stays robust while deposits lag, keeping LDR elevated. FCNR(B) deposit benefits likely visible only from Q2FY27

baroda-etrade-str.title: Automobiles: Q1FY27 Preview – Cost inflation drags margins; demand momentum a respite
baroda-etrade-str.description: Volume growth across segments healthy, driven by small cars in 4W and premium/scooter segment in 2W. Hyundai volume falls YoY/QoQ

baroda-etrade-str.title: Real Estate (Flex-Work): Q1FY27 Preview – Robust demand for Flex; improved utilisation to drive growth
baroda-etrade-str.description: Flex-Workspace operators — now the largest cohort of occupiers — accounted for ~27% of the total office leasing

baroda-etrade-str.title: Consumer Durables: Q1FY27 Preview – Cooling demand accelerates amid rising copper prices
baroda-etrade-str.description: Extended summer to drive cooling-led demand off a weak base, with refrigerators and washing machines also strong

baroda-etrade-str.title: Real Estate (REITs): Q1FY27 Preview – Supportive macro-environment; set up for strong performance
baroda-etrade-str.description: Record office absorption (~25msf, +22.2% YoY) and supply (~21msf, +22.8% YoY); preference for high-quality workspaces

baroda-etrade-str.title: Metals & Mining: Q1FY27 Preview – Higher Steel prices to drive profitability and support margins
baroda-etrade-str.description: Demand momentum to continue with a likely 7.0-8.0%YoY growth, driven by strong demand from construction and auto sectors

baroda-etrade-str.title: Consumer Staples: Q1FY27 Preview – Gradual recovery
baroda-etrade-str.description: Q1FY27E is likely to be a quarter of selective growth and gradual margin recovery across the Consumer sector

baroda-etrade-str.title: Automobiles: Momentum continues for now; monsoon stays a key concern
baroda-etrade-str.description: Entry segment demand tapers off MoM in PVs; 2W commuter space faces headwinds; Premiumisation trend across remains indifferent

baroda-etrade-str.title: Metals & Mining: Steel demand resilient; Prices under near term pressure
baroda-etrade-str.description: Demand growth at 7–9% CAGR; near-term prices weighed down by elevated inventories and increased imports

baroda-etrade-str.title: EMS: Q4FY26 Review – Demand recovery in sight; margin quality a near-term drag
baroda-etrade-str.description: RAC weakness on weather/BEE destocking masked channel normalisation; early sell-through and hikes set up the FY27 rebound

icici-bank-research.title: Morning Market Starter
icici-bank-research.description: India’s wholesale price inflation (WPI) accelerated to a 42-month high of 8.3% YoY in April from 3.88% YoY in March, driven by a sharp rise in crude oil, natural gas, food and fuel prices alongside a favourable base effect. Read the report to know more.

ing-think-rss.title: Low June inflation highlights Poland’s resilience despite past energy shock
ing-think-rss.description: CENTRAL AND EASTERN EUROPE: Lower fuel and food prices brought Poland’s CPI inflation back to target in June, with little evidence of spillovers from the previous energy shock. Inflation should stay below 3% this year but renewed Middle East tensions leave little room for rate cuts and call for a ca

ing-think-rss.title: National Bank of Hungary preview: Next, please!
ing-think-rss.description: CENTRAL AND EASTERN EUROPE: The next step of Hungary’s “mini rate cut cycle” is around the corner. Recent market turmoil hasn’t derailed the positive risk premium story overall, and the latest inflation figure was lower than the central bank projected. Markets, forever fixated on whatever comes next

ing-think-rss.title: Eurozone industrial production ticked down in May
ing-think-rss.description: Industrial production in the eurozone continues to trudge along at a sluggish pace without clear direction. The Middle East war has not caused a large decline in production, but recent optimism is not yet reflected in strength in output either

ing-think-rss.title: Rates Spark: EUR rates cannot follow a dovish US
ing-think-rss.description: Euro rate dynamics remain separated from the US, which is looking more at domestic factors, while oil and gas are primarily driving ECB pricing. Eurozone inflation may take longer to turn, so while both 2y USD and EUR rates look bullish, the US leg could come down first

ing-think-rss.title: FX Daily: Benign CPI takes sting out of dollar’s upside
ing-think-rss.description: The DXY dollar index sold off 0.5% on yesterday's benign US CPI print and 10bp came out of the expected Fed tightening cycle. Fed officials look to be on the same page, requiring several soft inflation prints to avoid tightening, but the June data must be welcome. Today's focus will be PPI and furt

ing-think-rss.title: China slowdown worse than expected on weak domestic demand
ing-think-rss.description: ASIA/PACIFIC: China's growth slowed to 4.3% year-on-year in the second quarter, the slowest quarterly pace since the pandemic. Though June saw better industrial production and retail sales, the investment slump worsened. Weak domestic demand also weighed on overall momentum. Growth remains within th

ing-think-rss.title: Poland’s current account deficit narrows in May amid softer imports
ing-think-rss.description: POLAND: Poland’s external imbalance came in below our forecast and market expectations as import growth fell short of forecasts. The external imbalance remains narrow despite the higher cost of energy imports as volumes have declined. The services surplus has stabilised after years of improvement

ing-think-rss.title: Easing US price pressure dampen imminent Fed hike talk
ing-think-rss.description: NORTH AMERICA: US June consumer price inflation data undershot expectations with the breadth of the softening the particularly encouraging aspect. Fed rate hike pricing has receded, but we still think it is too high. We forecast a prolonged pause from the Federal Reserve

ing-think-rss.title: FX Daily: Dollar upside risks are rising rapidly
ing-think-rss.description: Crude has jumped, but still isn’t fully pricing in a fully-fledged new supply shock, meaning short-term risks remain on the upside for the dollar. So far, the rate differential is keeping EUR/USD afloat thanks to hawkish repricing of ECB expectations alongside the Fed’s, but further energy price in

ing-think-rss.title: Monitoring Hungary: Is it time to be optimistic again?

The sector has opportunities for revenue growth linked to defense, global communications, and AI, according to Goldman Sachs FICC and Equities.

goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Markets] The tension between large market gains in AI stocks and friendly economic and corporate fundamentals continues to sharpen, according to Goldman Sachs Research.
